items.q: What is a self-fulfilling prophecy in education?
items.correct: When teachers' expectations (often based on labels) influence pupil behaviour and achievement — pupils labelled as 'bright' tend to do better, those labelled 'thick' worse, regardless of underlying ability (Rosenthal and Jacobson).
items.misconception: A self-fulfilling prophecy is when a student predicts their own grade correctly.
items.random_wrong: A self-fulfilling prophecy is when teachers predict the future accurately.
items.why: The mechanism is the LABEL changing teacher behaviour, which changes pupil outcomes — not students predicting themselves. Students miss the causal chain (label → interaction → outcome) that makes the concept analytically powerful.

items.q: What is Merton's strain theory of crime?
items.correct: Crime results from a mismatch between cultural goals (success, wealth) and legitimate means of achieving them — those who can't access legitimate means may innovate (crime), ritualise, retreat or rebel.
items.misconception: Strain theory says people commit crime because they are stressed.
items.random_wrong: Strain theory says criminals have psychological strain disorders.
items.why: 'Strain' is structural (gap between goals and means), not psychological stress. Students take the everyday meaning of strain. Merton's theory locates crime in social structure, specifically blocked opportunities.

items.correct: Early learning of norms and values, primarily within the family.
items.misconception: The most important socialisation, happening in school.
items.why: Students confuse 'primary' with primary school. Primary = first/early (family); secondary = later (school, peers, media). The terms describe ORDER, not importance or location.

items.q: Weber's concept for empathetic understanding of social action is:
items.why: Correct answer: Verstehen. “Anomie” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which research method involves in-depth participant observation?
items.why: Correct answer: Ethnography. “Survey” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Durkheim publish 'Suicide'?
items.why: The real figure is 1897 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
